openmeter/watermill/driver/noop.purpose: Provides a no-op implementation of the Watermill message.Publisher interface for use when event publishing must be disabled (e.g., tests, credits-disabled wiring, or feature-flagged paths). Satisfies the compiler without sending any messages.

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertion
patterns.description: var _ message.Publisher = (*Publisher)(nil) at package level ensures Publisher always satisfies the Watermill message.Publisher interface; add this line for any new interface implementation in this package.

key_file_guides.role: Entire package — defines the zero-value Publisher struct with Publish and Close methods that silently succeed.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add state, logging, or metrics here; this must be a true no-op. Never return errors from Publish or Close.

anti_patterns: Adding logging or metrics to the noop publisher — defeats the purpose and adds unwanted side-effects in disabled paths
Returning non-nil errors from Publish or Close — callers treat errors as real failures
Adding constructor functions that accept dependencies — the zero-value struct is the intended usage
Growing this package beyond the single Publisher type — other noop concerns belong in their own packages

decisions.rationale: noop.Publisher{} can be used directly without construction, making Wire provider functions trivially simple when publishing is disabled (e.g. credits.enabled=false paths).

code_examples.scenario: Wire provider returning noop publisher when feature is disabled
